title: Ganga River Rafting in Rishikesh
description: Rishikesh is home to some of the finest white-water rapids in the world. Navigate the emerald waters of the Ganga as it cuts through the Shivalik hills, led by professional river captains.

items.title: Rapids for Everyone:
items.text: From beginner-friendly Grade II rapids (like Club House and Double Trouble) to challenging Grade III+ rapids (like Roller Coaster and Golf Course).

items.title: Body Surfing:
items.text: Experience the thrill of floating in the calm stretches of the river under the supervision of your guide.

items.title: Cliff Jumping:
items.text: Leap off ledges into the deep river pool at designated points along the rafting route.

extraContent:
Safety & Regulations: Every raft is guided by a certified river captain and accompanied by a safety kayaker. High-buoyancy life jackets, helmets, and a detailed safety briefing are mandatory.

Best Time: Late September to mid-June. Rafting is closed during monsoons (July to mid-Sept).

title: Guided Wilderness Trekking
description: Walk through ancient oak forests, rhododendron groves, and high-altitude alpine meadows (bugyals). Trekking in Uttarakhand offers routes for every level of fitness.

items.title: Easy (Beginner-Friendly):
items.text: Deoriatal-Chandrashila trek, Nag Tibba. Perfect for families and weekend trips.

items.title: Moderate (Basic fitness):
items.text: Har Ki Dun, Valley of Flowers, Dayara Bugyal.

items.title: Difficult (High Altitude):
items.text: Roopkund, Kedartal, Bali Pass. Recommended for experienced trekkers.

extraContent: What to Expect: Walk with experienced local guides who understand trail safety, mountain weather signs, and flora/fauna. Mules or porters handle the luggage so you can walk comfortably.

title: India's Highest Bungee Jumping
description: Leap from India's highest fixed-platform bungee jumping site in Rishikesh. Jump from a cantilever platform built over a rocky cliff overlooking a tributary of the Ganga, at a height of 83 meters.
Designed and operated by jumpmasters from Australia and New Zealand. All harnesses, cords, and safety systems comply with international safety regulations.

items.question: Is there an age/weight limit?
items.answer: Minimum age is 12 years. Minimum weight is 35kg, maximum is 110kg.

items.question: Are there any medical restrictions?
items.answer: Jumping is not permitted for individuals with heart conditions, epilepsy, high blood pressure, back/neck injuries, or pregnancy.

title: Tandem Paragliding Joyrides
description: Soar like a Himalayan eagle over the valleys of Rishikesh or the alpine lakes of Bhimtal. Enjoy the panoramic views of terraced fields and winding rivers under the wings.
In a tandem flight, you are securely harnessed to an experienced pilot who handles the launch, navigation, and landing. You simply take a short run off the slope.

items.title: Certified Pilots:
items.text: Fly with licenses holding veteran pilots with hundreds of logged flight hours.

items.title: Equipment Checks:
items.text: Pre-flight glider checks and reserve parachutes are standard for all launches.

items.title: Ideal Season:
items.text: September to November, and March to May are best for flight thermals.
